fuzzer_role.value_profile: supporting context — shows CMP_MAP gradient mechanism in isolation
fuzzer_role.naive: supporting context — high-variance stochastic regime, orthogonal to the vpc/cmp story

expected_direction: vpc > cmp; ratio (vpc/cmp) grows monotonically with COST_INNER

expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile_cmplog: graceful degradation, never reaches zero (CMP_MAP gradient carries through high pollute)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.cmplog: monotone exponential decay in COST_INNER (I2S dictionary diluted to uselessness)
expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile: small but persistent across the entire range (CMP_MAP gradient alone, no I2S yield)

acceptance.rule: vpc median > 0 at COST_INNER=4096 AND cmp median = 0 at COST_INNER=4096 AND cmp's curve monotone non-increasing

design_basis: Sequential 4-step opcode chain with N=64 noise CMP table. COST_INNER controls how many noise CMPs are invoked per execution. cmp's I2S substitution rate compounds to (K/(K+N))^K over the chain — exponential dilution. vpc adds value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback on top of cmplog's I2S; the gradient is dilution-immune (pollute CMPs add static no-gradient entries) and carries vpc through where cmp dies.
notes: Primary comparison: vpc vs cmp. They differ by ONE component (CMP_MAP added to cmplog) — the cleanest matched-pair test of the synergy hypothesis.
COST_INNER is COMPILE-TIME parameter; one Dockerfile per scan value at /home/miao/libafl_fuzzbench/docker/targets/Dockerfile.b_chain_pollute_c{0,1,2,3}.
c0=0, c1=64, c3=512, c2=4096 — note that c2 and c3 names are swapped relative to natural ordering (c2 was the first overshoot built; c3 added later for smoother curve).
Existing fuzzbench targets directory: /home/miao/libafl_fuzzbench/targets/b_chain_pollute_c{0,1,2,3}/. The .c files in those dirs are byte-identical to this template.c.
